name: restraint-under-pressure
ground_truth_note: REDESIGNED after four curation rounds proved 'exactly zero compounds' unwinnable inside one cohesive codebase: every hand-curated 'independent' set turned out to encode a real (or accidentally planted) coupling, which the sweeper grounded legitimately. What mechanically separated the BAD baseline outputs (thematic umbrellas: 'copy has no owner', 'mutation scope masks tests') from good ones: compound count under pressure, grounded constituent-anchored evidence, and explicit rejection reasoning for non-grouped findings. Semantic admission-test adjudication (does the compound require the constituents' STATED defects?) is the grader-model second stage. Archived evidence: skill-evals/archive/IS-2-v1-*, IS-2-v2-*.
